requirements: 3 or 4-year bachelor's degree in Finance or Banking with a minimum of 68–75% from a recognised university is required. Alternatively, applicants holding a bachelor's degree in a relevant field may also be considered if their degree includes at least 20% of academic business studies, or if they have completed a minimum of 35 ECTS credits of academic business studies outside the degree. These studies must include courses in one or more of the following areas: corporate finance, investments, derivatives, financial management, financial markets, financial reporting, banking, or financial modeling.

name: Master’s in Economics
requirements: 3 or 4-year bachelor's degree in Banking, Econometrics, Economics, or Finance with a minimum of 68–75% from a recognised university is required. Alternatively, applicants holding a bachelor's degree in a relevant field may be considered if their degree includes at least 20% of academic economics/business studies, or if they have completed a minimum of 35 ECTS credits of academic economics/business studies outside the degree. These studies must include at least 25 ECTS in one or several of the following: economics, microeconomics, macroeconomics, managerial economics, international economics, financial markets, public economics, banking, econometrics, time-series analysis. Or the bachelor's degree that includes at least 35 ECTS (or equivalent) of academic business studies, of which minimum 25 ECTS must consist of economics, finance or financial management will be considered.

name: Master’s in Accounting and Auditing
requirements: 3 or 4-year bachelor's degree in Accounting, Auditing, Business Analytics, Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) and Sustainability, Finance with a minimum of 68–75% from a recognised university is required. Alternatively, applicants holding a bachelor's degree in a relevant field may also be considered if their degree includes at least 20% of academic business studies, or if they have completed a minimum of 35 ECTS credits of academic business studies outside the degree. These studies must include courses in one or more of the following areas: financial accounting, management accounting, auditing, internal audit, financial reporting, corporate sustainability reporting and business analytics.

name: Bachelor’s in Business Administration
requirements: Grade 12 with the following requirements for Indian students: CBSE/CISCE/Maharashtra Boards: 84% overall & 80% in 12th Maths, or 90% in 10th Maths (CBSE/CISCE), West Bengal Board: 74% overall & 70% in 12th Maths, or 90% in 10th Maths (CBSE/CISCE), & Other State Boards: 89% overall & 80% in 12th Maths, or 90% in 10th Maths (CBSE/CISCE).

name: Master's in Strategic Business Development
requirements: 3 or 4-year bachelor's degree in Accounting, Auditing, Banking, Brand Management, Business Administration, Business Analytics, Business Law, Consumer Behavior, Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) and Sustainability, Cross-Cultural Management, Digital Marketing, Economics, Entrepreneurship, Finance, Human Resource Management, Industrial Management (business degree), Innovation and Product Development, International Business, International Marketing, Management, Marketing, Operations Management, Organizational Behavior, Organizational Leadership, Project Management, Sales Management, Strategic Management, Supply Chain Management with a minimum of 68–75% from a recognised university is required. Alternatively, applicants holding a bachelor's degree in a relevant field may also be considered if their degree includes at least 20% of academic business studies, or if they have completed a minimum of 35 ECTS credits of academic business studies outside the degree. These studies must consist of courses in one or several of the following fields: accounting and finance, HR management, strategic management, international business, entrepreneurship and marketing.

name: Master's in Computing Sciences - Automation and Robotics
requirements: 3 or 4 Years bachelor's degree with minimum 63-65% in Computer/Electrical/Electronic(s)/Mechatronics/Mechatronical/Network/Power Systems/Software/Telecommunications/Computer Science/Industrial/Manufacturing/Production/Supply Chain Management/Systems/Automation/Energy/Renewable Energy/Robotics/Control Engineering, Data Science, Cyber Security, Information Technology, Computing, Digital Technology from a recognised university. name: Master's in Smart Energy Transition - Energy Engineering
requirements: 3 or 4 Years bachelor's degree with minimum 63-65% from a recognised university in Engineering: Process, Environmental, Geotechnical, Mining, Structural, Transport, Electrical, Electronic(s), Mechatronics/Mechatronical, Network, Power systems, Manufacturing/production, Safety, Systems, Acoustical/sound, Aeronautical, Automation, Astronautical, Automotive, Aviation, Energy, Marine, Ocean, Production technology or Machine shop, Renewable energy, Robotics, Thermal science, Nuclear, Petroleum. Alternatively, applicants holding a bachelor's degree with wide knowledge of engineering mathematics, physics and thermodynamics, plus understanding of technical/drawings/documentation, fundamental knowledge of chemistry and object-oriented programming may also be considered.

name: Master's in Smart Energy Transition - Electrical Engineering
